quick_skim.title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
quick_skim.abstract: This work presents a novel approach for adapting neural network architecture along the depth based on a posteriori error estimation. By formulating neural network training as a continuous-time optimal control problem, we derive rigorous error estimates that quantify how approximation error distributes across network layers. This error decomposition enables a principled depth adaptation strategy: new layers are inserted at locations of maximum estimated error, allowing the network to efficiently capture complex, nonlinear variations in the underlying problem. Our framework introduces a novel network architecture that treats weights and biases as piecewise linear functions varying across layers, with the error estimator bounding the discrepancy between this discrete representation and the true continuous optimal control solution. The approach leverages dual weighted residual methodology from finite element analysis to derive computable upper bounds on the functional error. A key theoretical contribution is the derivation of explicit error bounds that decompose the total approximation error into interval-wise contributions, providing a rigorous basis for targeted architecture refinement. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on scientific datasets, including learning the observable-to-parameter map for the Navier-Stokes equation. Numerical results reveal that our approach consistently outperforms existing architecture adaptation methods in terms of generalization performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimal control approach for neural network depth adaptation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于最优控制的神经网络深度自适应方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于最优控制的神经网络深度自适应方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于最优控制的神经网络深度自适应方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a novel method for adapting network depth using optimal control and a posteriori error estimation, inserting layers where error is maximal to efficiently capture complex variations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文将神经网络训练建模为连续时间最优控制问题，推导出后验误差估计以量化各层近似误差。基于误差分布，提出在误差最大处插入新层的深度自适应策略，并引入权值偏置逐层线性变化的新型架构，高效捕捉非线性变化。该方法为自动设计高效、紧凑网络提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文将神经网络训练建模为连续时间最优控制问题，推导出后验误差估计以量化各层近似误差。基于误差分布，提出在误差最大处插入新层的深度自适应策略，并引入权值偏置逐层线性变化的新型架构，高效捕捉非线性变化。该方法为自动设计高效、紧凑网络提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我找新的能用于通信建模或者均衡的神经网络结构，比如之前的PINN和KAN可以更efficient, especially work that connects or combines: novel neural network architectures; neural networks for communication channel modeling; neural network based equalization; Kolmogorov-Arnold networks in communication; efficient neural network designs for communication; deep learning techniques applied to communications; Find novel neural network architectures for communication channel modeling and equalization; Search for physics informed neural networks in communication systems; Discover efficient neural network structures for adaptive equalization.

quick_skim.title: Deep Neural Variation Spaces: A Unifying Perspective on Depth and Complexity
quick_skim.abstract: We develop a unified function space theory of deep fully connected neural networks. Functions in our spaces are defined recursively as $\ell^1$-bounded linear combinations of activated functions from preceding layers, with a dictionary of affine functions at the first layer. Unlike existing theories that are largely specialized to homogeneous activations such as the ReLU, our framework provides a meaningful notion of functional complexity for deep networks with a broad range of homogeneous and non-homogeneous activation functions commonly used in practice. This simple construction unites several seemingly disparate ideas from the literature, including norm-based complexity bounds and variational characterizations of depth, and facilitates novel analyses of what kinds of functions deep norm-constrained networks can represent. To this end, we prove a novel representer theorem for our spaces and establish novel function-space complexity bounds showing that the associated function classes remain qualitatively small at arbitrary depth. In the univariate ReLU case, we prove a "depth saturation" result: depth in this setting yields only a small constant rescaling of the function class, with no added functional diversity. As a consequence, we show that deep norm-controlled ReLU functions in any dimension cannot exhibit high frequencies along any direction. This finding reveals that some commonly cited expressivity benefits of depth disappear once network complexity is controlled by an appropriate function space norm, rather than parameter count or other representational costs that permit compounded rescaling across layers. Overall, our results illustrate how a function space perspective yields new structural insights into the relationship between depth and complexity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: theoretical framework for deep neural network function spaces
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 深度神经网络函数空间的统一理论
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 深度神经网络函数空间的统一理论
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 深度神经网络函数空间的统一理论
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops a unified function space theory for fully connected neural networks, providing a notion of functional complexity for a broad range of activation functions, unifying norm-based bounds and depth characterizations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文为深度全连接神经网络建立了统一的函数空间理论。不同于仅适用于特定激活函数的现有理论，该框架定义了一种递归式的函数复杂度度量，能够处理多种常见激活函数。它统一了范数复杂度界和深度变分刻画，为分析与设计新型网络架构提供了理论工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文为深度全连接神经网络建立了统一的函数空间理论。不同于仅适用于特定激活函数的现有理论，该框架定义了一种递归式的函数复杂度度量，能够处理多种常见激活函数。它统一了范数复杂度界和深度变分刻画，为分析与设计新型网络架构提供了理论工具。

quick_skim.title: Architecture Generalization with MetaNCA
quick_skim.abstract: Self-organization is an emergent property of life, driven by the collective behavior of individual components acting on local information. Biological neurons, through local interactions transmitted through synapses, are able to learn efficiently and can adapt their connections over an organism's lifespan. Motivated by these desirable properties of adaptability and local interaction, neural cellular automata (NCA) models have been successful at learning morphogenesis solely through local update rules, demonstrating stability over many updates and robustness to perturbations. In this work, we introduce Meta Neural Cellular Automata (MetaNCA), a framework that learns local rules which self-organize the weights of artificial neural networks. A learned rule network iteratively updates the weights of a task network using only local interactions on the computation graph. We propose a novel Weight Transformer architecture for the local rule network, which uses linear attention to aggregate signals from neighboring weights and hidden states. Once trained, the rule network generates task networks of diverse architectures without backpropagation. We show that MetaNCA generates weights for feedforward MLPs, CNNs, and ResNets on MNIST and CIFAR-100, scaling to networks of 2 million parameters. We further show that MetaNCA generalizes to architectures not seen during meta-training, and that architectural diversity in the training phase strengthens this generalization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes MetaNCA, a novel framework for self-organizing neural network weights
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出MetaNCA，一种自组织神经网络权重的新框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出MetaNCA，一种自组织神经网络权重的新框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出MetaNCA，一种自组织神经网络权重的新框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MetaNCA learns local rules to self-organize weights of neural networks, introducing a novel architecture generation method.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了元神经细胞自动机（MetaNCA），通过学习局部更新规则来自组织神经网络的权重，实现架构层面的泛化。虽然该方法属于新颖的神经网络架构训练范式，但未与通信信道建模或均衡任务结合，属于通用方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了元神经细胞自动机（MetaNCA），通过学习局部更新规则来自组织神经网络的权重，实现架构层面的泛化。虽然该方法属于新颖的神经网络架构训练范式，但未与通信信道建模或均衡任务结合，属于通用方法。
